response.reasoning: Step 1 — Years of experience: Freelance work starts 2006, professional roles from 2009; either way exceeds the 15-year floor. Check. Step 2 — Education: Two bachelor's degrees (CS and Fine Arts/Design). No master's or PhD in an AI-related field. Preferred education not met. Step 3 — Seniority level: Most recent titles are 'Staff Software Engineer' and 'Senior Software Developer.' The role is explicitly a director-level individual contributor. There is no evidence of operating at that organizational level — no P&L influence, no cross-org technical strategy ownership, no executive stakeholder advisory work. Significant gap. Step 4 — AI expertise: The current role (re:cinq, ~1 year) mentions AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators. This is entry-level AI tooling exposure, not recognized expertise with demonstrated AI products or platforms at scale. No ML frameworks, no LLM product ownership, no published AI work in the resume. Step 5 — Cloud expertise: AWS CDK appears at Optilyz in an application engineering context. No cloud platform strategy, no cloud architecture at scale. Step 6 — Entrepreneurial signal: GISCollective is a founder-led open-source geo-data project running since 2016. Positive signal but the scope is a small NGO/OSS tool, not a venture-scale or intrapreneurial initiative. Step 7 — Strategic advisory: Zero evidence of advising customers or partners on technology adoption, inspiring cross-org initiatives, or shaping product direction at a strategic level. Step 8 — Overall: Solid full-stack craftsperson with good breadth, but the role is an AI-strategy thought leader operating at director level. The candidate is several career steps and an entire AI domain away from that profile.
strengths: 20 years of continuous software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — comfortably clears the experience floor
Founder of GISCollective and contributor to open-source tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) show genuine builder initiative beyond day-job scope
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq indicates awareness of and interest in the agentic AI space
concerns: Title and scope are senior/staff IC, well below the director-level individual contributor seniority the role requires
AI expertise is shallow and very recent — no ML background, no demonstrated AI products at scale, no recognition in the AI community
No evidence of strategic advisory work, executive stakeholder influence, or shaping technology direction at an organizational or industry level
response.justification: The candidate is a capable and versatile software engineer with two decades of hands-on experience, but the Principal Engineer (AI Strategy) role demands a director-level IC with deep, recognized AI expertise and a track record of advising organizations on cloud and AI strategy at scale. The candidate operates at senior/staff IC level, has only surface-level and very recent AI exposure, and shows no history of strategic advisory, executive influence, or thought leadership in AI. The gap is structural, not a matter of polish.
